ATM / SQX Reference Widget

Complete reference for the Algo Trading Masterclass (Ali/ATM), SQX Masterclass, and SQX Platform courses. All 143 lessons with full transcripts, notes, and cross-references.

🔵 Ali/ATM — The WHY & Methodology 🟠 SQX Platform — The HOW & Where to Click 🟢 SQX Masterclass — Practical Walkthroughs

⚡ Known SQX Bugs — Always Check These

  • M207 Slippage field BROKEN: Put combined commission+slippage in Commission field only. Leave slippage = 0.
  • M202/M207 Custom Sessions (v135.8): Session filters affect entries/exits only — indicators still calculate on ALL data. Workaround: export+import as new symbol.
  • M404 Template Import: Sometimes fails to link random groups. Fix: import XML first → create group manually → re-link.
  • M409 EasyLanguage: 'Use InitialStopLoss' appears twice — comment one out with //
  • M409 Lookback bug: Indicator lookback >50 bars → manually set "Maximum bars study references" in Strategy Properties.
  • Data License Error: After changing license → Mass Delete → Clear Data (NOT Delete) → Update Selected.

Market Edges Database
REFERENCE168 markets · StatOasis_Market_Edges_v4.5.xlsx
168 markets
Symbol ⇅ Asset ⇅ Classification ⇅ BO Rank ⇅ BO P&L ⇅ MR Rank ⇅ MR P&L ⇅ MR Side Notes
How to read: Data since 2007 (Futures) / 2005 (FX/Stocks/ETFs). P&L = 1 contract / 1 lot / 100 shares. Higher MR Rank = better mean reversion. Higher BO Rank = better breakout. They are inverse of each other.
BO Strong Breakout (≥35)
BO-MOD Moderate BO (25–34)
MIXED Neutral
MR-MOD Moderate MR (25–34)
MR Strong MR (≥35)
⚠️ Discrepancy: GBP/NZD and GBP/CAD — M106 lesson describes as MR but Excel data shows strong MR (ranks 38-39). The Excel data-driven classification takes precedence. Flagged with ⚠️.

EXACT WORDING
ATM vs SQX Masterclass — Key Differences
🔵 Ali/ATM vs 🟢 SQX Masterclass 29 differences across 6 categories
These differences appear as 🔑 callout boxes in the Full Notes of each relevant lesson. This page consolidates all of them in one place for quick reference. Neither approach is universally correct — they reflect different priorities and starting points. Understanding why each course makes its choice is more valuable than picking a side.
Custom Project Pipelines
🟢 SQX Masterclass 🔵 Ali/ATM Task sequences for automated strategy building
Custom Projects in SQX automate the full build+robustness pipeline. SQX runs each task sequentially — strategies flow from one databank to the next, with failed strategies deleted at each stage. Only survivors reach the final databank. Critical: "Delete failed strategies from databank" must be ON in every retest task.
Gold XAUUSD H1 SQX Masterclass · S10 Config file: XAUUSD-H1.zip
Build window: Jan 2009 – Jun 2019  |  Pre-2009 gold has low volatility, no telling value  |  Stop order required, SL + PT required  |  Spread: 60 pips, Slippage: 10 pips
TASK 1
Build Strategies
Data: Jan 2009 – Jun 2019
Precision: OHLC
Spread: 60 pips
Slippage: 10 pips
Target: 1,000 strategies
→ Save to: Results
TASK 2
OOS 1
Data: Jul 2019 → latest
Precision: M1
PF ≥ 1.0
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: OOS1
TASK 3
OOS 2
Data: Jan 2006 – Dec 2008
Precision: M1
PF ≥ 1.1
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: OOS2
TASK 4
M30 Test
Data: Full range
Timeframe: M30
PF ≥ 1.1
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: M30
TASK 5
H4 Test
Data: Full range
Timeframe: H4
PF ≥ 1.0
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: H4
TASK 6
Slippage Test
Data: Full range, H1
Slippage: 100 pips
PF ≥ 1.0
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: Slippage
TASK 7
MC Parameters
Randomise Params: ON
Probability: 30%
Max change: 30%
Simulations: 200
Precision: M1
R/DD MC ≥ 50% of original
→ Save to: MC Final
TASK 8
Clear + Loop
Clear: Results, OOS1,
OOS2, M30, H4, Slippage
Keep: MC Final
→ Go to Task 1
Repeat indefinitely
GBPJPY H1 & CFDs SQX Masterclass · S10 Config file: GBPJPY-custom_project.zip
Simplified 3-task pipeline vs Gold's 8 tasks  |  Cross-test markets: EURJPY + USDJPY  |  Faster loops = more strategies per hour
TASK 1
Build Strategies
Data: 2009 – 2018
Timeframe: H1
Target: 1,000 strategies
→ Save to: Results
TASK 2
All Tests Combined
OOS1: Sep 2018 → latest, PF ≥ 1.0
OOS2: 2003 – 2008, PF ≥ 1.1
EURJPY: full range, PF ≥ 1.0
USDJPY: full range, PF ≥ 1.0
Slippage: +5 pips, PF ≥ 1.0
All via Additional Markets cross-check
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: Tests
TASK 3
MC Parameters
Randomise Params: ON
Probability: 30%
Max change: 30%
Simulations: 200
R/DD MC ≥ 50% of original
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: MC Final
TASK 4
Clear + Loop
Clear: Results, Tests
Keep: MC Final
→ Go to Task 1
Repeat indefinitely
ATM Class Breakout (NQ/ES) Ali/ATM · M407 Config: clone and swap symbol only
WFO → WFM → Final Verdict pipeline  |  5 cycles for randomisation  |  Chain multiple projects back-to-back by symbol
TASK 0
Clear Databanks
Clear: Results, WFO, WFM
Keep: Final Verdict
Runs at start of each cycle
TASK 1
Build Strategies
Data: 2008 – Dec 2017
Template or random
Target: 200 per cycle
(1,000 total over 5 cycles)
→ Save to: Results
TASK 2
WFO
Data: All (2008 → 2020)
Variation: ±20%, step 10
200 strategies, floating %
Pass: ≥70% profitable,
OOS/IS stability >50%
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: WFO
TASK 3
WFM
OOS%: 17–23, step 3
IS runs: 7–13, step 3
5,000 strategies
Floating %, slower
Pass: 7/9 cells, 100% score
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: WFM
TASK 4
Final Verdict
Data: All + OOS (last yr)
No cross-check
Final verdict filters loaded
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: Final Verdict
TASK 5
Loop × 5
Go to Task 0
Condition: cycles < 5
Each cycle = 200 strats
Total = 1,000 generated
TASK 6
Save Files
Save: SQX format
Export: databank as XLSX
Export: EasyLanguage (.ELD)
Folder: organised by symbol
TASK 7
Clear All
Clear ALL databanks
including Final Verdict
Ready for next project
ATM Class Daily Mean Reversion (ES) Ali/ATM · M408
Uses SPP instead of WFM — more appropriate for daily timeframe strategies with fewer trades  |  Trade threshold lowered to 120
TASK 0
Clear Databanks
Clear: Results, SPP
Keep: Final Verdict
TASK 1
Build Strategies
Data: 2007 – Dec 2017
Timeframe: Daily
Direction: Long MR
Limit orders
Trades ≥ 120 (lowered)
Target: 205 per cycle
→ Save to: Results
TASK 2
SPP
Data: All (2007 → 2020)
Max variations: 5,000
Pass: 70% profitable,
annual return <135% median
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: SPP
TASK 3
Final Verdict
Data: All + OOS (last yr)
No cross-check
Final verdict filters
Delete failed: ON
→ Save to: Final Verdict
TASK 4
Loop × 5 then Save
Go to Task 0 if cycles < 5
After 5 cycles: Save Files
Export XLSX + ELD
Clear all databanks
ATM Manual Development Pipeline Ali/ATM · M309 The full strategy development workflow
Applies to any platform — not just SQX automated projects  |  Screenshot at M309 ~00:07:52 shows the full diagram  |  Build window: Jan 2008 – Jan 2018 (futures)
STEP 1
Data Setup
M1 precision data
Jan 2007 start (futures)
Session configured
Commission = $0
(add later)
STEP 2
Builder
Signals + indicators
+ timeframe blocks
+ session blocks
+ order types
→ Results databank
STEP 3
Ranking Filters
IS: avg trade >$50
IS: trades >83, PF >1.1
OOS: avg trade >70% IS
OOS: trades >70% IS
Full: PF >1.1, R/DD >1.5
STEP 4
Candidates Bank
Add commission now
Manual visual review
Uniform yearly P&L
Remove outlier-only strats
STEP 5
Robustness
Manual: 1-2 variable
WFO → WFM
or SPP (daily strats)
MC simulations
All must pass
STEP 6
Final Verdict
PF >1.0
R/DD >1.0
OOS >70% of IS
≥25 trades total
≥25 trades holdout
STEP 7
Portfolio
Min 3, optimal 10
Correlation <0.5
Diversify by style
+ TF + sector
Use QA4 Pro
ONGOING
Bench
Keep building
replacement strats
When one fails
monitoring → swap
Never run dry
⚡ Critical Settings for ALL Projects
"Delete failed strategies from databank" must be ON in every retest task. If off, all strategies flow to the next databank regardless of pass/fail — results become meaningless.
Clone an existing project and change only the building template + symbol. All thresholds, task structure, and robustness settings inherit automatically.
Key Thresholds
🔵 ATM🟢 SQX MC

🔵 ATM Robustness

  • WFM AA (3×3)7/9 pass
  • WFM AAA (3×3)8-9/9 pass
  • WFM AA (5×5)18/25 pass
  • WFM AAA (5×5)21+/25 pass
  • WFO profitable runs≥70%
  • OOS/IS stability>50%
  • SPP AAA annual return<100% median
  • SPP AA annual return<135% median
  • Final OOS vs IS≥70%
  • Min trades (final)≥25
  • Min trades (holdout)≥25 or 1yr
  • Final verdict PF>1.0
  • Final verdict R/DD>1.0

🟢 SQX MC Robustness

  • WFM pass7/9 ≥50% eff.
  • MC R/DD (95%)≥50% original
  • MC sims minimum200
  • Min trades intraday/yr≥30
  • Min trades daily/yr≥70
  • IS+OOS build PF≥1.3
  • Other tests PF≥1.1
  • Other market PF≥1.0
  • Win rate IS+OOS≥30%
  • R/DD per year≥0.5
  • Opt Report pass≥70%
  • 2nd OOS PF≥1.1

🔵 ATM Avg Trade Minimums

  • Formula minimum2.5 × RT cost
  • Formula excellent4 × RT cost
  • ES (~$28 RT)$70 / $112
  • Gold (~$50 RT)$100 / $200
  • Nat Gas (~$40 RT)$100 / $160
  • EMD (~$50 RT)$125 / $200

🟢 SL/PT Ranges by Timeframe

  • M15 / M3015–100 pips
  • H130–200 pips
  • H4 / D150–300 pips
  • Gold600–3,000 pips
Robustness Checklist
🟢 SQX MC Ordered Sequence

🟢 SQX MC — Essential Tests (in order)

  • Second OOS test (data before build window) — PF ≥1.1, R/DD ≥0.3/yr
  • Slippage test — add 3 pips extra, must remain profitable (PF ≥1.0). Skip for scalping.
  • Test on another market ⭐ MOST IMPORTANT — PF ≥1.0 on correlated market
  • Test on different timeframe — one step shorter + one step longer
  • MC Randomise trades order (exact method)
  • MC Randomise strategy parameters ⭐ MOST IMPORTANT MC TEST
  • MC Randomise history data (volatility/broker sensitivity)
  • Walk Forward Matrix — 7/9 cells ≥50% effectiveness in 3×3
  • Last OOS test + recent year filter — M1 precision, PF ≥1.0

Bonus Tests (informative, not mandatory)

  • MC Resampling (trades order)
  • MC Skip trades (~10% skipped)
  • MC Slippage + spread combined
  • MC Overall combined test
  • 3D charts test (parameter pairs)
  • MAE/MFE (critical for no-SL strategies)
  • Optimisation Report (≥70% = robust)
  • SPP (best for trending markets)

Cross-Test Market Pairs

  • EUR/USD ↔ GBP/USD or USD/CHF
  • GBP/USD ↔ EUR/USD
  • EUR/JPY ↔ GBP/JPY
  • AUD/CAD ↔ AUD/CHF
  • S&P 500 ↔ Dow Jones
  • Gold = no comparable (skip test)
IBS Quick Reference
🔵 ALI/ATMBonus lesson + M212, M402, M403, M410

Formula & Range

  • Formula(Close−Low)÷(H−L)
  • Rangealways 0 to 1
  • IBS = 0close at low (sellers)
  • IBS = 1close at high (buyers)
  • IBS >0.75strength (buyers)
  • IBS <0.25weakness (sellers)
  • Best holding period1 day to 1 week

US Index Behaviour

  • Low IBS = BUY opportunity on US indexes
  • Mean reversion tendency strong at extremes
  • IBSMBN-bar synthetic IBS
  • 12-bar > 5-bar > 2-bar in quality
  • IBS Body Strength|C−O|÷(H−L)
  • Body Strength highconviction bar

SQX Custom Blocks

  • IBS Craw value 0–1
  • IBS > levelcondition block
  • IBS < levelcondition block
  • IBSMBmulti-bar synthetic
  • Import from XML inCustomize→Blocks
Market Regime Quick Reference
🔵 ALI/ATMBonus lesson + M215

Direction Indicator (1–5)

  • 1Extreme Bear
  • 2Bear
  • 3Sideways
  • 4Bull
  • 5Extreme Bull

Volatility Indicator (1–4)

  • 1Very Quiet
  • 2Quiet
  • 3Volatile
  • 4Extreme Volatile

Key Rules

  • Recommended methodMethod 6
  • Method 6 regimes6 (3dir × 2vol)
  • Best RSI-2 regimeDir3 + Vol4
  • Best RSI-2 win rate76%
  • Min trades/regime25–30
  • Values are FIXEDnever optimise

Method 6 Groupings

  • Direction Bearstates 1+2
  • Direction Sidewaysstate 3
  • Direction Bullstates 4+5
  • Volatility Quietstates 1+2
  • Volatility Activestates 3+4
  • S&P bear market~23% of time
Portfolio Rules
🔵 ALI/ATMM501, M502, M309

Portfolio Size

  • Minimum strategies3
  • Optimal strategies10
  • Maximum strategies20
  • After 20no more benefit
  • MC simulations1,000–5,000
  • MC confidence98%

Diversification Axes

  • Strategy directionLong / Short
  • Strategy styleMR + BO + Seasonal
  • Timeframeintraday + swing
  • Holding periodintraday + overnight
  • Instrument sectorenergy/metals/indices
  • Correlation target<0.5 between strats

Capitalisation Formulas

  • Method 1MC DD ÷ target DD%
  • Method 2MC DD + all margins
  • MC DD vs backtestoften 2× higher
  • Data start (futures)Jan 2007
  • Holdout startJan 2018

Naming Convention

  • [SYM]_[L/S/LS]_[TYPE]_[N] - [NAME] - [TF]
  • ExampleES_L_MR_001 - RSI2 - H1
  • TYPE optionsMR / BO / SEA
  • Store bysymbol folder

Screenshots to Capture
🔵 ALI/ATM 52 screenshots · capture from course videos at the timestamps shown
Work through this list in one session — scrub to the timestamp in the course video, capture, and save as the lesson ID + number (e.g. m207_1.png). Once captured, screenshots can be added to each lesson by replacing the placeholder divs with <img> tags.
